+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-154
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_14-59-53+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-154_TASK_2026-05-24_14-47-18+0300.md — TASK packet being executed (read-only)
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project — fixture manifest inspected (Q1)
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/scenes/main.rge-scene — fixture scene inspected (Q1)
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md — fixture intent doc inspected (Q1)
+- crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs — existing schema-only test inspected (Q1, Q2)
+- crates/rge-data/Cargo.toml — dep surface inspected (Q2, Q3)
+- crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs — public surface inspected (Q2)
+- crates/rge-data/src/project.rs — Project schema inspected (Q1, Q2)
+- crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs — Scene schema inspected (Q1, Q2)
+- crates/rge-data/src/entity_ref.rs — EntityId(Ulid) shape inspected (Q3)
+- kernel/ecs/Cargo.toml, kernel/ecs/src/lib.rs, kernel/ecs/src/world.rs, kernel/ecs/src/entity.rs — kernel ECS surface inspected (Q2, Q3, Q4)
+- kernel/app/Cargo.toml, kernel/app/src/lib.rs, kernel/app/src/app.rs — kernel App / phase runner inspected (Q2)
+- kernel/events/Cargo.toml, kernel/schedule/Cargo.toml, kernel/plugin-host/Cargo.toml — kernel substrate deps inspected (Q2)
+- crates/editor-shell/Cargo.toml, crates/editor-shell/src/lib.rs, crates/editor-shell/src/world.rs — editor-shell surface inspected (Q2)
+- editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml, editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs — editor binary surface inspected (Q2)
+- crates/script-host/Cargo.toml — script-host dep surface inspected (Q2)
+- crates/script-bench/Cargo.toml — script-bench dep surface inspected (Q2)
+- Cargo.toml — workspace member + dependency edges inspected (Q2, Q3)
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-154_TASK_2026-05-24_14-47-18+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. Audit-only dispatch.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None. Audit-only dispatch.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. Audit-only dispatch.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Only this EXEC report (and its `.meta.json` sidecar, written by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`) is added by this dispatch. No tracked source / test / Cargo / workflow / script / doc / golden-project / packet file was edited or added.
+
+## 5-Question Load+Tick Preflight Answer Block
+
+### Q1. Current fixture
+
+After task #34, `golden-projects/simple-scene/` carries exactly three tracked files plus the existing schema-only validator. Concrete facts and evidence:
+
+- Manifest: `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` (8 lines) declares
+  `Project(version: "0.1.0", name: "simple-scene", description: "Basic load + transform + camera + light render", target_tiers: [desktop], plugins: [], scenes: ["scenes/main.rge-scene"])` — `.rge-project:1-8`.
+- Scene fixture: `golden-projects/simple-scene/scenes/main.rge-scene` (15 lines) declares
+  `Scene(version: "0.1.0", name: "main", entities: [Entity(id: "0000000000000G000000000000", name: "Camera", components: [], relations: [])], root_entities: ["0000000000000G000000000000"])` — `main.rge-scene:1-15`. The scene is intentionally untyped (empty `components` and `relations`) per the schema-only contract.
+- README intent: `golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md:1-6` declares the directory is the W21/PLAN §13 "Basic load + transform + camera + light render" regression harness; nothing in the fixture currently exercises that — it is the future surface.
+- Schema-only validation test: `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:46-63` parses the manifest as `rge_data::Project` and asserts `version == SchemaVersion::V0_1_0`, `name == "simple-scene"`, non-empty description, `target_tiers == [Desktop]`, `plugins.is_empty()`, and `scenes == [ScenePath("scenes/main.rge-scene")]`. `golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:83-116` parses the referenced scene as `rge_data::Scene` and asserts `version == V0_1_0`, `name == "main"`, non-empty entities + root_entities, every root id resolves via `Scene::find_entity`, and the first root is named "Camera" with empty `components` / `relations`. The negative-path tests at `golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:66-80` and `:119-138` confirm that removing the required `name` field from either file breaks RON parsing. The doc-comment header at `golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:1-10` is explicit: this test asserts only schema facts, no renderer / GPU / asset / cook / screenshot / typed component bridging.
+
+No additional simple-scene file is tracked; no other test references the fixture (verified via grep for `simple-scene` across the workspace — only the planner-side `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and the existing schema test reference it).
+
+### Q2. Load/tick consumers
+
+Inspection method: grep `rge_data|rge-data` across the whole repo (returned 59 matches, of which only `crates/rge-data/**`, `tools/schema-diff/Cargo.toml`, `crates/components-identity/src/lib.rs`, `crates/io-gltf/src/lib.rs`, `crates/asset-store/**`, `crates/pak-format/src/header.rs`, doctrine docs, and dispatch logs hit) and per-crate `Cargo.toml` review for every required inspection surface. Classifications per the TASK packet's required labels:
+
+- `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` — `not-a-consumer`. It is the input data, not a code path. Evidence: `.rge-project:1-8` (data only).
+- `golden-projects/simple-scene/scenes/main.rge-scene` — `not-a-consumer`. Same reason. Evidence: `main.rge-scene:1-15`.
+- `crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` + `crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs` — `schema-only`. They define the wire shape (`Project` at `project.rs:27-44`, `Scene` at `scene.rs:33-45`, plus `Entity` / `ComponentValue` / `Relation` at `scene.rs:67-115`). No constructor lifts these into any tickable runtime structure; the only convenience method is `Scene::find_entity` at `scene.rs:62-64` (a linear scan in the data crate). `Scene` itself has no `World`-typed method anywhere.
+- `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` — `schema-only`. Confirmed at `golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:46-138`; this is the only test that already consumes the fixture, and it stops at RON → struct parsing with structural asserts.
+- `crates/rge-data/tests/round_trip.rs`, `tests/schema_validation.rs`, `tests/migration_test.rs` — `schema-only`. They exercise serialization round-trip and the migration registry on synthetic Project / Scene fixtures; none builds a World. Evidence: their only cross-crate import is `use rge_data::…` (grep `use rge_data`: `schema_validation.rs:6`, `round_trip.rs:14-15`, `migration_test.rs:14-15`, `golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:15`, `bake_fixtures.rs:14` — all stay inside `rge_data`).
+- `crates/rge-data/Cargo.toml` — `schema-only`. Its only production cross-crate dep is `rge-kernel-asset` (`Cargo.toml:13`); `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-kernel-app`, `rge-kernel-schedule`, `rge-kernel-events`, `rge-kernel-plugin-host` are absent from both `[dependencies]` and `[dev-dependencies]`.
+- `kernel/ecs/**` — `not-a-consumer`. Exposes a tickable `World` (`world.rs:52-70`, `pub fn new` at `world.rs:75`, `pub fn spawn` at `world.rs:121`, `pub fn spawn_with_id` at `world.rs:133`, `pub fn advance_tick` at `world.rs:111-114`, `pub fn current_tick` at `world.rs:94`, `pub fn entity_count` at `world.rs:211`) and its own ULID-backed `EntityId` (`entity.rs:22`, `EntityId::from_ulid` at `entity.rs:37-39`). `kernel/ecs/Cargo.toml:12-17` lists `ulid`, `thiserror`, `tracing`, `serde`, `postcard` — no edge to `rge-data`. Grep `rge_data|rge-data` under `kernel/ecs/` returns no matches. Tickable, but not connected to current `rge_data::Project` / `rge_data::Scene` loading.
+- `kernel/app/**` — `not-a-consumer`. Provides the per-frame driver `App::run_frame(frame_dt, sink, phase_runner)` at `app.rs:153-189` and the `FramePhase::ALL` ordering invoked by tests at `app.rs:235-243`; `Cargo.toml:12-15` depends only on `rge-kernel-diagnostics` + `serde`. No `rge-data` edge; grep for `rge_data` under `kernel/app/` returns no matches.
+- `kernel/events/**`, `kernel/schedule/**`, `kernel/plugin-host/**` — `not-a-consumer`. Each `Cargo.toml` (`kernel/events/Cargo.toml:13-15`, `kernel/schedule/Cargo.toml:13-15`, `kernel/plugin-host/Cargo.toml:13-16`) depends on `rge-kernel-diagnostics` plus serde / thiserror / tracing only. Grep `rge_data|rge-data` across all of `kernel/` returns zero matches.
+- `crates/editor-shell/**` — `not-a-consumer`. `Cargo.toml:19-71` enumerates the deps (`winit`, `tracing`, `rge-editor-state`, `rge-editor-actions`, `rge-editor-egui-host`, `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-input`, `rge-cad-projection`, `rge-gfx`, `rge-brep-render`, `rge-cad-core`, `glam`, `ulid`, `wgpu`) with no `rge-data` entry; dev-deps at `Cargo.toml:88-94` are only `serde` / `ron`. `src/world.rs:30-63` wraps a `rge_kernel_ecs::World` plus a legacy blob storage but does not parse `.rge-project` / `.rge-scene` files anywhere. Grep `rge_data|rge-data` under `crates/editor-shell/` returns exactly one hit, a comment line in the test fixture at `tests/fixtures/scene_100_entities.ron:1-10` ("The fixture format mirrors what `rge-data` (W14) will eventually emit") — confirmed not a code path. The W03 PIE snapshot loader at the same path parses its own RON shape, not `rge_data::Scene`.
+- `editor/rge-editor/**` — `different-scene-type`. `Cargo.toml:16-42` lists `rge-editor-shell`, `rge-cad-core`, `rge-cad-projection`, `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-io-gltf`, `rge-brep-render`, `glam`, `winit`, `tracing*`, `notify`; no `rge-data`. `src/main.rs:78` imports `use rge_io_gltf::{import_glb, Cache, Entity, MemoryCache, Scene, Transform}` — the `Scene` used at `main.rs:931`, `:1000`, `:1042`, `:1093`, `:1411`, `:2399` is `rge_io_gltf::Scene` (constructed via `Scene::new()` and populated with `EntityComponents { name, transform, parent, mesh, material, skeleton }`), a distinct type from `rge_data::Scene`. Grep `rge_data` under `editor/` returns no matches.
+- `crates/script-host/**` — `not-a-consumer`. `Cargo.toml:12-24` deps are `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-kernel-events`, `rge-kernel-diagnostics`, `rge-kernel-types`, `wasmtime`, `serde`, `thiserror`, `tracing`, `ron`, `blake3` — no `rge-data`. Grep `rge_data|rge-data` across `crates/script-host/` returns no matches. The crate exposes a WASM ↔ ECS bridge (`src/ecs_bridge.rs`), not a `rge_data::Scene` consumer.
+- `crates/script-bench/**` — `not-a-consumer`. `Cargo.toml:12-40` deps are `rge-kernel-diagnostics`, `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-kernel-events`, `rge-script-host`, `serde`, `serde_json`, `memory-stats`, `wasmtime`, `wat` — no `rge-data`. Grep `rge_data|rge-data` across `crates/script-bench/` returns no matches. The crate's `engine_stub.rs` / `workloads.rs` / `native_baseline.rs` define synthetic benchmark workloads, distinct from any current simple-scene consumer.
+
+No source file or test under the required surfaces is classified `usable-now` or `renderer-only`.
+
+### Q3. Scene->World bridge
+
+**No.** A direct `rge_data::Scene` → `rge_kernel_ecs::World` bridge does not exist anywhere in the workspace.
+
+Evidence:
+- `crates/rge-data/Cargo.toml:12-17` lists no edge to `rge-kernel-ecs` (or any kernel crate except `rge-kernel-asset`); `crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs:1-172` has no method or free function that constructs or fills a `rge_kernel_ecs::World`.
+- Grep `rge_data` under `kernel/` returns no matches, and `kernel/ecs/src/world.rs` / `kernel/ecs/src/entity.rs` carry no `rge_data` import — the kernel does not reverse-depend on the data crate.
+- Grep for an editor or runtime call site that takes a `rge_data::Scene` and emits a `rge_kernel_ecs::World` returned exactly zero non-test, non-data-crate matches (the only `use rge_data::...` import sites are all inside `crates/rge-data/{tests,examples}` itself).
+
+Smallest bridge shape needed before any meaningful load+tick assertion can run (description only — no code in this dispatch):
+
+- Input: `&rge_data::Scene` (the structure parsed by the existing schema test).
+- Output: a freshly constructed `rge_kernel_ecs::World` (or a `&mut World` filled in place).
+- Body: for each `scene.entities[i]`, call `world.spawn_with_id(rge_kernel_ecs::EntityId::from_ulid(entity.id.0))` (compatible because `rge_data::EntityId` at `entity_ref.rs:36-38` is `pub EntityId(pub Ulid)` and `rge_kernel_ecs::EntityId::from_ulid` at `entity.rs:37-39` accepts the same `ulid::Ulid`).
+- Scope of the bridge for the current simple-scene fixture: identity-only. Both `components` and `relations` are empty in `main.rge-scene:4-11`, so no typed component bridging, asset lookup, or relation storage is reachable yet — the minimal bridge is a one-loop function.
+- Where it should live is a separate architectural question and is not decided here. Plausible locations (each with cost): a dev-only test helper file under `crates/rge-data/tests/` adding `rge-kernel-ecs` as a `[dev-dependencies]` entry (smallest); a production module on `rge-data` adding `rge-kernel-ecs` to `[dependencies]` (broadens the schema crate); a new bridge crate `rge-scene-loader` (workspace member churn); kernel/ecs growing a `rge-data` dep (kernel-tier inversion, not appropriate).
+
+### Q4. GPU-free assertions
+
+With the workspace as it stands today — i.e. with no `rge_data::Scene` → `rge_kernel_ecs::World` bridge per Q3 — there is no meaningful renderer-free, GPU-free *load+tick* regression beyond what `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` already pins. The existing schema test (`golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:46-138`) covers parse → struct asserts; "tick" is not reachable without a bridge that produces a `World` (or an equivalent tickable container) instance.
+
+Once the minimal bridge from Q3 exists (identity-only, ULID copy), and still respecting the renderer / GPU / asset-store / cook / screenshot / typed-component / editor-UI exclusions, the meaningful GPU-free assertions on the current fixture become:
+
+- After the bridge runs on the parsed Scene, `world.entity_count()` (`kernel/ecs/src/world.rs:211`) equals `scene.entities.len()` (== 1 for the current fixture).
+- Each `scene.entities[i].id` resolves to a live entity in the world (existence probe via the public surface in `kernel/ecs/src/entity.rs` / `world.rs`).
+- `let t0 = world.current_tick(); world.advance_tick(); assert_eq!(world.current_tick(), t0 + 1);` — exercises the canonical tick counter from `world.rs:94` / `world.rs:111-114` and confirms the loaded world is tickable.
+- Optionally `assert!(world.last_tick() == t0)` after the same advance to pin the change-detection contract (also free, same surface).
+
+These do not touch `kernel/asset`, `kernel/asset-streaming`, `kernel/gfx`, `crates/asset-store`, `crates/asset-pipeline`, `crates/build-pipeline`, `crates/io-*`, the editor egui host, any wgpu surface, or any typed `Component` registration — they use only `rge_data` parsing + `rge_kernel_ecs::{World, EntityId}` public surface that already exists today.
+
+### Q5. Next dispatch or NEEDS_HUMAN
+
+Proposed next dispatch (a single, bounded, smallest-safe step):
+
+- **Title (suggested):** "ISSUE-155 simple-scene minimal load+tick test"
+- **Goal:** Add the smallest GPU-free load+tick regression for `golden-projects/simple-scene` by introducing a test-local identity-only `rge_data::Scene` → `rge_kernel_ecs::World` bridge in a new integration test under `crates/rge-data/tests/`, plus the matching `[dev-dependencies]` entry for `rge-kernel-ecs` on `rge-data`. No production code path, no production cross-crate dep, no renderer / GPU / asset / cook / screenshot / typed-component / editor-UI surface touched.
+- **Allowed file edits:**
+  - Add `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_load_tick.rs` (new file). The test:
+    1. Reads and parses `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` as `rge_data::Project` (same loader pattern as `golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:17-29`).
+    2. Resolves the first scene path and parses the referenced scene as `rge_data::Scene` (same pattern as `golden_simple_scene_schema.rs:31-43`).
+    3. Constructs `let mut world = rge_kernel_ecs::World::new();` and, for each entity in `scene.entities`, calls `world.spawn_with_id(rge_kernel_ecs::EntityId::from_ulid(entity.id.0))`.
+    4. Asserts `world.entity_count() == scene.entities.len()`.
+    5. Captures `let t0 = world.current_tick();`, calls `world.advance_tick();`, asserts `world.current_tick() == t0 + 1` and `world.last_tick() == t0`.
+  - Edit `crates/rge-data/Cargo.toml`: add exactly one line under `[dev-dependencies]` — `rge-kernel-ecs = { workspace = true }`. No other Cargo change.
+- **MUST NOT touch:** any file under `golden-projects/**`; any other test in `crates/rge-data/tests/`; any file under `kernel/**`, `runtime/**`, `editor/**`, or any other production source; any other `Cargo.toml`; `Cargo.lock` lines beyond what the dev-dep addition mechanically forces; workflows under `.github/**`; PowerShell scripts; doctrine, status, planning, ADR, or architecture docs; any existing handoff packet; GitHub issue / PR / branch / label / remote state except the queue runner's normal lifecycle.
+- **Focused gates (Executor-side):**
+  - `cargo build -p rge-data --tests` → exit 0
+  - `cargo test -p rge-data --test golden_simple_scene_load_tick` → all green
+  - `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-data` → exit 0
+  - `cargo clippy -p rge-data --tests --no-deps -- -D warnings` → exit 0
+- **Canonical gates (queue-runner-side):**
+  - `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` → all green
+  - `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0
+  - `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0
+- **Halt conditions for that dispatch:** any need to add a production cross-crate dep beyond the single `[dev-dependencies]` entry on `rge-data`; any need to touch typed components, asset storage, renderer / GPU resources, cook output, screenshot baselines, or editor UI; any architecture-lint violation that would require widening a kernel-tier dep edge; any failure that demands editing `golden-projects/**` or any kernel / editor source; any conflict between the W03 PIE snapshot path and the new test. On any of these, halt and write the EXEC with `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+Why this is the smallest safe follow-up rather than `NEEDS_HUMAN`: per Q3, the only design choice is "where does the bridge live", and the smallest envelope that yields a real load+tick assertion is a *test-local* bridge inside `crates/rge-data/tests/` plus a single dev-dep. This deliberately does **not** ship a production `rge-data` → `rge-kernel-ecs` edge, does not create a new workspace member, does not inject the bridge into kernel/ecs (which would invert the tier direction), and does not pre-empt the future "real" bridge crate decision — it just lets the simple-scene golden-project gain a meaningful regression. The same architectural decision the planner would face for a production bridge stays open after this dispatch.
+
+Recommendation: ISSUE-155 simple-scene minimal load+tick test (test-local identity-only Scene→World bridge in `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_load_tick.rs` + single `[dev-dependencies] rge-kernel-ecs` line in `crates/rge-data/Cargo.toml`, asserts entity_count and advance_tick on the parsed simple-scene fixture; no production source / renderer / GPU / asset / cook / screenshot / typed-component / editor-UI surface touched).
+

+STATUS: OPEN

+## Goal
+
+Perform GitHub issue #154 as a read-only preflight audit for the next golden
+simple-scene evolution step after task #34. Task #34 landed the first
+schema-load-only `golden-projects/simple-scene` fixture: a manifest with one
+scene path, a current-schema `.rge-scene`, and an `rge-data` integration test
+that parses both. This dispatch must determine whether the repository already
+has a usable load+tick path from `rge_data::Project` / `rge_data::Scene` into a
+runtime or editor-tick structure, whether a direct `rge_data::Scene` to
+`rge_kernel_ecs::World` bridge exists, what GPU-free assertions would be
+meaningful, and exactly one smallest safe follow-up dispatch or `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any source file under `crates/**`, `kernel/**`, `runtime/**`, `editor/**`, or
+  any other production source directory.
+- Any test file, example file, fixture, generated asset, or data file.
+- Any file under `golden-projects/**`, including the simple-scene manifest,
+  README, and scene fixture.
+- Any Cargo file, including `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace manifests,
+  generated Cargo metadata, or dependency declarations.
+- Any `.github/**` workflow file.
+- Any PowerShell script, shell script, schema, architecture lint, doctrine
+  document, status document, planning document, ADR, architecture document,
+  README, or root-level repository document.
+- Any existing handoff packet or existing dispatch log, including this TASK
+  packet after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, review, comment, commit, remote state,
+  or queue metadata except the queue runner's normal issue lifecycle for this
+  dispatch.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-154_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-154_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if
+  `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+- This dispatch's own queue-runner log under `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` only
+  if the queue runner creates it as part of normal dispatch operation.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, test files, examples, fixtures, golden-project files,
+  generated binary assets, screenshot baselines, cook output, renderer
+  comparison artifacts, docs, architecture docs, Cargo files, dependency
+  files, schemas, scripts, workflows, architecture lints, TASK packets, REVIEW
+  packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+- Any handoff packet other than this dispatch's single `ISSUE-154_EXEC_*.md`
+  report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One read-only `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet for ISSUE-154.
+- The EXEC report contains the exact heading:
+  `## 5-Question Load+Tick Preflight Answer Block`
+- Under that heading, the EXEC report answers Q1 through Q5 with these exact
+  Markdown heading lines:
+  - `### Q1. Current fixture`
+  - `### Q2. Load/tick consumers`
+  - `### Q3. Scene->World bridge`
+  - `### Q4. GPU-free assertions`
+  - `### Q5. Next dispatch or NEEDS_HUMAN`
+- Q1 states exactly what `golden-projects/simple-scene` contains after task
+  #34, including the manifest, scene path, scene fixture, and the schema-only
+  facts currently validated by `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs`.
+  It must cite file/line evidence for each claim.
+- Q2 enumerates the plausible current or candidate load+tick paths found under
+  the required inspection surfaces and classifies each path as exactly one of
+  `usable-now`, `schema-only`, `different-scene-type`, `renderer-only`, or
+  `not-a-consumer`. Each classification must include file/line evidence and a
+  short reason.
+- Q3 states whether a direct `rge_data::Scene` to `rge_kernel_ecs::World`
+  bridge already exists. This answer must be decided before Q5 recommends any
+  implementation. If no bridge exists, Q3 describes the smallest bridge shape
+  that would be needed before load+tick can be meaningful, without writing code
+  or choosing a broader architecture.
+- Q4 states what a renderer-free, GPU-free load+tick regression could assert on
+  the current simple-scene fixture without typed component bridging, asset
+  loading, renderer resources, editor UI, cook output, or screenshot baselines.
+  If no meaningful load+tick assertion exists until a bridge is added, Q4 must
+  say so plainly with evidence.
+- Q5 recommends exactly one smallest safe follow-up dispatch or exactly
+  `NEEDS_HUMAN`. It must include one final line beginning with
+  `Recommendation: ` followed by exactly one concrete follow-up dispatch or
+  exactly `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The EXEC report includes a concise command log for read-only file searches,
+  file reads, and status checks used to support Q1-Q5.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The dispatch is read-only except for its own generated EXEC report, optional
+  matching sidecar, and possible queue-runner log.
+- The EXEC report includes the exact five-question block heading and exact

+  scene fixture, README intent if relevant, and existing schema-only validation

+  ECS/events/schedule/plugin-host, editor-shell/editor, and
+  script-host/script-bench.
+- Q2 uses only these classification labels for candidate load+tick paths:

+- Q4 stays renderer-free and GPU-free. It must not rely on asset-store

+## Constraints / Non-Goals

+- Do not add or edit a `rge_data::Scene` to `rge_kernel_ecs::World` bridge.
+- Do not add or edit tests, fixtures, examples, source, Cargo files, scripts,

+MUST classify candidate load+tick paths as usable-now, schema-only, different-scene-type, renderer-only, or not-a-consumer
+MUST determine whether a direct rge_data::Scene to rge_kernel_ecs::World bridge exists before recommending implementation
+MUST recommend exactly one smallest safe follow-up dispatch or NEEDS_HUMAN
+MUST halt rather than fix if the audit reveals an implementation requirement outside the read-only scope
+```
